name: K-Scale Labs
tagline: Open-source humanoid robotics startup building affordable K-Bot robots
description: Founded in 2024 by ex-Meta/Tesla engineer Benjamin Bolte, K-Scale Labs raised ~$4.5M (including a $4M seed round and Y Combinator W24 backing) to build open-source humanoid robots priced under $10,000. The company shipped a limited Founder's Edition of the K-Bot and had over $2M in pre-orders, but shut down in November 2025 after failing to find a lead investor for the capital needed to scale manufacturing. Bolte open-sourced all IP under non-commercial licenses, calling it a foundation for 'future hackers and dreamers.'
story: K-Scale Labs wanted to do for humanoid robots what Arduino did for microcontrollers — make them open, hackable, and cheap enough for anyone to build on. Founded in 2024 by Benjamin Bolte, a former Meta and Tesla engineer, the company raised $4.5 million including a $4 million seed round and Y Combinator W24 backing. The pitch was compelling: a full humanoid robot priced under $10,000, with all hardware and software open-sourced. K-Scale shipped a limited Founder's Edition of the K-Bot and racked up over $2 million in pre-orders. But building affordable hardware at scale requires the kind of capital that seed funding cannot provide, and the company needed a lead investor willing to bet big on open-source robotics — a category that makes venture capitalists nervous about defensibility. When no lead materialized, Bolte shut down operations in November 2025, canceled all pre-orders, and open-sourced the company's entire IP under non-commercial licenses. He called it a foundation for 'future hackers and dreamers.' The technology survived; the company did not.

sources.title: K-Scale Labs Cancels K-Bot Orders, Open-Sources All IP After Funding Fails — Humanoids Daily
Palo Alto Humanoid Startup K-Scale Labs Shuts Down — Mike Kalil
K-Scale Labs CEO Explains Shutdown: I Bet the Farm on the K-Bot Launch — Humanoids Daily
